chapter_title.ar: باب الْعَصِيرِ الَّذِي قَدْ مَسَّتْهُ النَّارُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 27 – The (grape) juice which the fire has touched

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/282)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/282)
text: كُلُّ عَصِيرٍ أَصَابَتْهُ النَّارُ فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ حَتَّى يَذْهَبَ ثُلُثَاهُ وَ يَبْقَى ثُلُثُهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Every juice hit by the fire so it is Prohibited, until two-thirds of it goes away and there remains a third’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn Mahbub from ‘ Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Any juice (grape juice) which fire has touched is unlawful to use until two-thirds of it is evaporated and only one-third has remained.’”
